name: THE GRAPPLER — STRONGEST TEEN PROTOCOL
combat_style: All-around striker-grappler — calisthenics mastery + boxing power + karate/kung-fu striking + grappling + grip
fantasy: Chase the monsters of the underground arena — build absurd bodyweight strength, an iron core, and a fighter's whole toolbox, all through real, safe training.
tagline: Fight the phantom. Chase the ogre.
core_rule: Chase the monster, train like a human.
core_rule_enforcement: Every prescription is real, scaled, and safe. The anime feats (Demon Back, super-steroids, titanium teeth, 30-hour training, bare-handed bear kills) are lore and imagery ONLY — they appear as flavor with a fantasy tag, never as goals, and are never reproduced as training content.
sourcing_note: Grounded in the deep-research report (saved at protocol-src/research/baki-research-report.md). Fan/creator structure from SuperheroJacked's Baki calisthenics program (Upper/Lower/Full-Body split + HIIT + dedicated core + shadow-boxing for the 'phantom' theme) and its per-character library (Katsumi karate, Retsu kung fu, Hanayama grip). Canon character facts from the Baki wiki. NO claims were refuted, but the research explicitly FLAGGED as non-trainable fantasy: Jack's PEDs/titanium teeth/30-hr training, Yujiro's genetic Demon Back, and all superhuman feats — these are handled as lore only. Makiwara/knuckle 'conditioning' safety was NOT well-sourced, so this campaign replaces bare-knuckle board conditioning with safe heavy-bag work + wrist/structure alignment and flags makiwara as traditional-but-injury-risky.
phantom_motif: Baki's signature imaginary-opponent 'phantom' fighting maps to shadow-boxing/visualization. Fight rounds flagged with a 'phantom' goal are shadow-fights against an imagined opponent (react, counter, flow) and double as mental training. Completing every phantom round across the campaign earns THE PHANTOM achievement.

persona_progression.theme: Calisthenics base and the iron core — bodyweight mastery, first phantom rounds
Striking base — boxing power and explosiveness
One-punch power — karate striking (SAFE bag conditioning, not bare knuckles)
Iron grip and clinch — grip strength, carries, grappling control
Ground flow — solo grappling and submission drills
Raw size and power — heavy compound strength. FANTASY-FLAGGED: no PEDs, no extreme bulk, no 30-hr training
Primal functional strength — the hardest block begins
Everything integrated — the strongest of the arena, escalating survival
The father-son boss — all-around mastery + mental dominance, form/control gate

fight.requirements: basic strikes, stance work, solo drills; no maximal loads
combinations, phantom rounds, grip/grappling flow; bag recommended
high-output combinations, sustained phantom flow, advanced grappling; experienced athletes

safety_notes.striking_conditioning: Karate/kung-fu striking uses a HEAVY BAG with proper wrist/knuckle alignment and gradual volume — NOT bare-knuckle makiwara board striking. Makiwara knuckle 'conditioning' is traditional but carries real arthritis/joint-injury risk and is unnecessary for fitness; it is flagged and never prescribed.
safety_notes.ped_framing: The Jack Hanma stage is a raw size/power THEME only. Its lore (super-steroids, titanium teeth, 30-hour training) is shown with a fantasy tag and explicitly NOT reproduced. The stage prescribes normal, safe heavy-compound strength work.
safety_notes.fantasy_flags: Demon Back/Demon Face (genetic), titanium-teeth Biting Path, bare-handed bear kills, and all superhuman feats are lore/imagery with a '⚠ anime fantasy — not a training goal' tag.
safety_notes.overtraining: High-volume calisthenics and HIIT stages cap total volume, keep predetermined targets, and never prescribe max-until-collapse.

equipment_profiles.rule: Every stage must support at least the MINIMAL version. Calisthenics-first means most stages run on a pull-up bar + floor; grappling stages need only a mat/floor and solo drills (partner always optional).

achievements.name: The Phantom
achievements.trigger: Complete every phantom (shadow-fighting) round across the campaign

achievements.name: One-Punch Power
achievements.trigger: Clear the Katsumi karate stage (4) with form intact

achievements.name: Sea King
achievements.trigger: Clear the Retsu kung-fu stage (5) within target precision

achievements.name: Ground Flow
achievements.trigger: Clear the Izou jiu-jitsu stage (7) cleanly

achievements.name: Beat the Ogre
achievements.trigger: Defeat Stage 12 (Yujiro) without a control-breakdown fail

achievements.name: Strongest Teen
achievements.trigger: Clear Fit and Fight for all 12 stages

response: Reduce reps or use an easier progression
Preserve the Fight difficulty where appropriate
Drop Fight difficulty by one level
Do NOT automatically lower the Fit path

safety_stop: Hand/knuckle pain from striking (stop bag work)

final_boss.control_mechanic: The all-around test: twelve rounds cycling every skill of the campaign (strike, grapple, grip, phantom, power). Form/control is gated — output scores only while technique holds. Yujiro's edge is mental as much as physical, so composure counts: rushing and flailing fails the round.
round_objectives: Face the Ogre — stance, breath, composure
Phantom round — read and counter the imagined attack
Mental dominance — stay composed under fatigue
Survival round — hold form, capped output

fit_final_test.rule: Predetermined targets only — never max-until-collapse. Calisthenic arm volume capped.

art.art_note: Placeholder banner reuse — swap for an original Baki-inspired asset before launch (IP).
